spec_match.rationale: Tests exact fragment matching behavior using three DOM anchors with different capitalizations (section, Section, SECTION) and lights up the matched cell on hashchange.

interactive.rationale: Users can click the #section, #Section, and #SECTION navigation links to see which corresponding anchor lights up in real time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns perfectly with the specification by showing how Chrome 150 handles fragment matching across standards and quirks mode.

edge_cases.rationale: As the page contains a DOCTYPE and executes in standards mode, it cannot demonstrate actual quirks mode behavior directly, but it does query document.compatMode to inform the user.

design_system.rationale: Excellent responsive layout, consistent CSS classes, and fits seamlessly into the design style rules.

openQuestions.title: Implement quirks-mode simulation via iframe
openQuestions.detail: Like the anchor-matching-demo, fragment-tester runs in standards mode. Using an iframe loaded in quirks mode (no DOCTYPE) would make the comparison between the two modes fully interactive and show the Chrome 150 fix in action.

summary: A functional fragment matching tester that lets users visually verify case-sensitive anchor targeting on the active document, but cannot directly run in quirks mode.
